avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snow cover will continue to thaw but will remain generally well bonded. Greatest amounts will be found on South-West through North-West to North-East aspects above 600 metres. Any remaining cornices will be prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will rise well above the summits for this forecasted period. Occasional rain, mainly overnight and in the afternoon. Near gale winds, South-South-West in the morning, backing South in the afternoon.
Observed Weather Influences: With the freezing level around 800 metres overnight, precipitation initially fell as snow before turning to rain as the freezing levels climbed. Very strong winds from the West throughout the day with gusts regularly exceeding 50 mph.
ObservedSnowStability: The snowpack is wet and soft becoming firmer with altitude. New snow is very limited in distribution and has had little effect on the well bonded snow cover. There has been snow loss, particularly from South and South-East aspects, ridges and sub 600 metres. Stability of remaining cornices is poor. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: There will be a significant thaw throughout the period. The existing consolidated snowpack will become wet, and in general remain stable. However, some wet snow instability is possible on very steep ground above 850 metres on North through East to South aspects. Such activity is not expected to be significant but may lead to some small wet snow avalanches. Please also see comments below. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Overnight rain will become patchy in the morning but more persistent again during Tuesday afternoon. This rain will be to all levels. There will be gale force South-South-Westerly winds on the tops throughout the day. Freezing level will rise well above the summits overnight, and remain there throughout Tuesday.
Observed Weather Influences: It was a showery day with sleet and wet snow above 900 metres with a drier afternoon. Winds were strong South-Westerly on the summits. The freezing level has slowly risen to above the tops during the day.
ObservedSnowStability: The existing snowpack continues to consolidate and is stable. There is thawing and depletion at lower levels.  Greatest snow accumulations can be found above 800 metres, on North through East to Southerly aspects. Firmer snow with icy patches exist at higher elevations. The avalanche hazard is Low.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Localised wet snow instability on steep North to East aspects above 950m. Wet, weak and collapse prone cornices over some of these locations. Elsewhere the snowpack will be wet or moist but generally well stabilised. Some potential for minor full depth avalanches from very steep snow laden crags. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Wet overnight then showery during the day. Mild at all altitudes throughout.
Observed Weather Influences: Strong South West winds. Overnight snowfall but only near summits with rain at lower levels; drier later in the period. Freezing near summits but milder later.
ObservedSnowStability: Moist, moderately bonded windslab on steep North to East aspects but only above 1000m and generally near summit level. Limited wet surface snow instability noted around midday on some very steep slopes and gullies on the same aspects but at lower altitudes. Wet, weak cornices present over recent lee slopes. Elsewhere the snowpack is wet or moist but generally well stabilised.  The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will become very wet. Wet snow instabilities will be present on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. Cornices above these aspects will be prone to collapse. Stability will be worst overnight and during the morning. Below 1000 metres the wet snowpack will be better consolidated, and stability will be better. The avalanche hazard will be Considerable

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: A mild day with rain at times and the freezing level above the summits. Winds from the South.
Observed Weather Influences: Overnight the freezing level was around 1050 metres, rising to 1200 metres during the day. Snow overnight above 900 metre turned to rain below 1100 metres during the day. Wind were from the South-West.
ObservedSnowStability: Overnight significant quantities of fresh windslab have been deposited above 1000 metres on mainly North to East aspects. This was going through a period of instability as the freezing level rose during the morning. Fresh cornices above these aspects were unstable and prone to collapse. A number of avalanches, at least some of which where cornice triggered, occurred overnight and during the morning. The avalanche hazard is Considerable

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: Mild conditions with rain will further reduce the snow cover. Generally the snowpack in exposed areas will be soft and moist with reasonable stability. Wet snow instabilities will persist above 900 metres in wind sheltered gullies and on slopes with a North-West through North to Easterly aspect. Gully exits and corrie rims will be most affected. Fragile cornices may exist in these areas. The avalanche hazard will be Moderate.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: Mild conditions will affect the area with freezing levels rising to around 3000 metres. Rain at all levels overnight is expected to become lighter through the day. Winds will be strong, occasionally gale force, generally Southerly.
Observed Weather Influences: Rain, sleet with snow on the higher summits has affected the area. The freezing level is around 1050 metres and rising above summit levels. Winds have been strong to gale force, generally South-Westerly. Drifting onto lee slopes mainly above 900 metres has occurred.
ObservedSnowStability: Much of the snowpack on exposed slopes is soft moist and is generally stable. Deep accumulations of moderately bonded snow exist mainly above 950 metres in wind sheltered gullies and on slopes with a Northerly to Easterly aspect. Avalanche debris noted on an Easterly aspect. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.

avalancheActivity.comment: Forecast Snow Stability: The snowpack will be moist and thawing at all elevations but generally stable throughout. However, there will be isolated surface instabilities mostly above 1000 metres on North to East aspects primarily around coire rims and gully tops. Cornices will be unstable and prone to collapse. The avalanche hazard will be Low.

snowpackStructure.comment:
Forecast Weather Influences: The freezing level will rise from being around the summits to well above the summits, where it will remain for the duration. Only trace amounts of wet summit snow expected. Winds, generally Southerly, will be fresh initially then increasing through the night to become gale force.
Observed Weather Influences: The freezing level has steadily risen throughout the day to be around summit level. Near constant precipitation, at first falling as snow to around 900 metres but latterly becoming confined to the highest summits. Drier late afternoon. Very strong West-South-West winds decreased to become fresh.
ObservedSnowStability: Fresh moderately bonded windslab is building primarily on North to East aspects above 1000 metres. These deposits are mostly shallow and are confined to wind-sheltered locations such as gully tops and coire rims. Fragile cornices are developing in the same areas. Elsewhere the snowpack is generally stable. The avalanche hazard is Moderate.
